core.visualization_core.visualization¶

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_moment_curvature(m_c_res: MomentCurvatureResults, x=None, ax=None, title='', show_points: bool = False, show_ultimate_point: bool = False)[Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plot moment–curvature (M–K) diagram with My and Mu annotations.

Parameter:

  • m_c_res – MomentCurvatureResults object from structuralcodes library

  • x – Relative position along the Beam x∈[0,1]

  • ax – The axes with the plot.

  • title – Optional title

  • show_points – Optional flag to show each point

  • show_ultimate_point – Optional flag to highlight the last point

Rückgabe:

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_moment_curvature_with_reference(m_c_res: MomentCurvatureResults, ref_curvatures, ref_moments, x=None, ax=None, title='', ref_label='')[Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plot moment–curvature (M–K) diagram from m_c_res and superimpose a second M–K dataset given as curvature and moment lists.

Parameter:

  • m_c_res (MomentCurvatureResults) – Object containing chi_y and m_y arrays.

  • ref_curvatures (list or array) – Reference curvature values.

  • ref_moments (list or array) – Reference moment values.

  • x (float, optional) – Position factor for title.

  • ax (matplotlib.axes.Axes, optional) – Existing axes to plot on.

  • title (str, optional) – Plot title prefix.

  • ref_label (str, optional) – Label for reference dataset.

Rückgabe:

ax – The axes with the plot.

Rückgabetyp:

matplotlib.axes.Axes

class core.visualization_core.visualization.PlotLine(moments: list, curvatures: list, name: str, color: str = 'black', linestyle: str = 'solid', linewidth: float = 1.0, marker: str = '', markersize: float = 4.0)[Quellcode]¶

Bases: object

Author: Elliot Melcer Container for a single moment–curvature dataset to be plotted.

moments¶

Moment values [kNm].

Type:

list or array

curvatures¶

Curvature values.

Type:

list or array

name¶

Legend label for this line.

Type:

str

color¶

Matplotlib color string (e.g. „black“, „#FF0000“, „tab:blue“).

Type:

str

linestyle¶

Matplotlib linestyle string (e.g. „solid“, „dashed“, „dotted“, „dashdot“) or shorthand („-“, „–“, „:“, „-.“).

Type:

str

linewidth¶

Line width. Defaults to 1.0.

Type:

float

marker¶

Matplotlib marker string (e.g. „x“, „o“, „“). Defaults to „“.

Type:

str

markersize¶

Marker size. Defaults to 4.0.

Type:

float

moments: list¶
curvatures: list¶
name: str¶
color: str = 'black'¶
linestyle: str = 'solid'¶
linewidth: float = 1.0¶
marker: str = ''¶
markersize: float = 4.0¶
classmethod from_results(m_c_res: MomentCurvatureResults, name: str, color: str = 'black', linestyle: str = 'solid', linewidth: float = 1.0, marker: str = '', markersize: float = 4.0) → PlotLine[Quellcode]¶

Construct a PlotLine from a MomentCurvatureResults object, applying the standard unit conversions:

curvatures : chi_y → -chi_y * 1e6 [1/1000m] moments : m_y → -m_y / 1e6 [kNm]

Parameter:

  • m_c_res (MomentCurvatureResults) – Raw results object with chi_y [1/m] and m_y [Nm] arrays.

  • name (str) – Legend label for this line.

  • color – Forwarded directly to PlotLine.

  • linestyle – Forwarded directly to PlotLine.

  • linewidth – Forwarded directly to PlotLine.

  • marker – Forwarded directly to PlotLine.

  • markersize – Forwarded directly to PlotLine.

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_moment_curvature_multiple(lines: list[PlotLine], ax: Axes | None = None, title: str = '', x: float | None = None, xlabel: str = 'Krümmung κ [1/1000m]', ylabel: str = 'My [kNm]', xlim: tuple[float, float] | None = None, ylim: tuple[float, float] | None = None, xmarker: float = 5.0, ymarker: float = 5.0) → tuple[Figure, Axes][Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plot multiple moment–curvature (M–K) datasets on a single axes.

Parameters:
param lines:

One or more M–K datasets to plot, drawn in list order.

param ax:

Existing axes to plot on. A new figure/axes is created when omitted.

param title:

Plot title prefix.

param x:

Position factor appended to the title as "M-K-Diagram at x = {x} * L".

param xlabel:

Label for the horizontal axis. Defaults to "K [1/1000m]".

param ylabel:

Label for the vertical axis. Defaults to "My [kNm]".

param xlim:

(x_min, x_max) axis limits. If omitted, matplotlib auto-scales.

param ylim:

(y_min, y_max) axis limits. If omitted, matplotlib auto-scales.

param ymarker:

Marker spacing on x-axis

param xmarker:

Marker spacing on y-axis

Rückgabe:

ax – The axes with the finished plot.

Rückgabetyp:

matplotlib.axes.Axes

Verursacht:

ValueError – If lines is empty.

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_moment_curvature_multiple_and_differences(lines: list[PlotLine], title: str = '', x: float | None = None, xlabel: str = 'Krümmung κ [1/1000m]', ylabel: str = 'My [kNm]', ylabel_diff: str = '$\\mathrm{\\Delta M_y}$ [kNm]', xlim: tuple[float, float] | None = None, ylim: tuple[float, float] | None = None, ylim_diff: tuple[float, float] | None = None, main_height: float = 3.0, diff_height: float = 0.6, figsize: tuple[float, float] = (6.4, 8.0)) → tuple[Figure, Axes, list[Axes]][Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plot multiple moment–curvature (M–K) datasets on a primary axes, with one dedicated difference panel beneath it per comparison line.

Layout:

Row 0 : full M–K plot (all lines) Row 1 : ΔMy lines[0] − lines[-1] Row 2 : ΔMy lines[1] − lines[-1] …

lines[-1] is the reference. All panels share the x-axis. Differences are computed by linearly interpolating each comparison line onto lines[-1]‘s curvature grid; points outside a comparison line’s range are masked as NaN. A single annotation is placed to the right of the difference panels, vertically centred across all of them.

Parameter:

  • lines (list[PlotLine]) – At least two M–K datasets. lines[-1] is the reference.

  • title (str, optional) – Plot title prefix.

  • x (float, optional) – Position factor appended to the title.

  • xlabel (str, optional) – Label for the shared horizontal axis (bottom panel only).

  • ylabel (str, optional) – Label for the primary (moment) axis.

  • ylabel_diff (str, optional) – y-axis label for every difference panel.

  • xlim (tuple[float, float], optional) – x-axis limits shared across all panels.

  • ylim (tuple[float, float], optional) – y-axis limits for the primary panel.

  • ylim_diff (tuple[float, float], optional) – y-axis limits applied to every difference panel.

  • main_height (float, optional) – Relative height of the primary panel. Default 3.0.

  • diff_height (float, optional) – Relative height of each difference panel. Default 0.6.

  • figsize (tuple[float, float], optional) – Figure size (width, height) in inches. Default (6.4, 7.776), which is the matplotlib default width and 1.62x the default height.

Rückgabe:

  • fig (matplotlib.figure.Figure)

  • ax_main (matplotlib.axes.Axes) – The primary M–K axes (row 0).

  • ax_diffs (list[matplotlib.axes.Axes]) – One axes per comparison line, in the same order as lines[:-1].

Verursacht:

ValueError – If lines contains fewer than two entries.

core.visualization_core.visualization.table_moment_curvature(m_c_res: MomentCurvatureResults)[Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Return a tabulated string of moment–curvature results_c1_1.

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_constitutive_law_concrete(concrete: Concrete, n: int = 100, debug: bool = False, show_markers: bool = False)[Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plot the constitutive law (stress–strain curve) for this concrete material

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_constitutive_law_reinforcement(reinforcement: Reinforcement, n: int = 100)[Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plot the reinforcement stress–strain constitutive law.

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_cross_section(gs: GenericSection, ax=None, x=None, title='', **kwargs)[Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plot the section geometry, its centroid, and the local coordinate system.

Parameter:

  • ax (matplotlib.axes.Axes, optional) – Axis to draw on. If None, a new figure is created.

  • x (float, optional longitudinal coordinate for title.)

  • kwargs (dict) – Extra args passed to geometry.plot().

  • gs (GenericSection) – Generic section geometry.

  • title (str, optional) – Additional title of the plot.

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_triangulated_mesh(triangulated_data: List[Tuple[ndarray, ndarray, ndarray, ConstitutiveLaw]], show_centroids=True)[Quellcode]¶

Visualize triangulated fibers returned by FiberIntegrator.triangulate().

Parameter:

  • triangulated_data (list of tuples) – (x, y, area, constitutive_law)

  • show_centroids (bool) – If True, draw centroid dots.

core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_mesh_with_triangles(triangulated_data)[Quellcode]¶
core.visualization_core.visualization.plot_strain_profile(results: dict, title: str = None)[Quellcode]¶

Author: Elliot Melcer Plots Strain Profile for Moment Calculation Results

core.visualization_core.visualization.mirror_plot(lines: list[PlotLine], ax: Axes | None = None, title: str = '', xlabel: str = '', ylabel: str = '', xlim: tuple[float, float] | None = None, ylim: tuple[float, float] | None = None, xmarker: float = 5.0, ymarker: float = 5.0, figsize: tuple[float, float] = (12, 5), flip_y_axis: bool = False, coordinate_axes: bool = True, show_x_numbers: bool = True, show_x_ticks: bool = True, x_number_position: str = 'bottom', show_x_axis_label: bool = True, show_legend: bool = True, x_number_pad: float = 8.0, axis_arrows: bool = True, show_vertical_grid: bool = True, show_horizontal_grid: bool = True, limit_grid_to_xlim: bool = True, x_scale: float = 1.0, y_scale: float = 1.0) → tuple[Figure, Axes][Quellcode]¶
